title: Sour Rye Pickle
description: A comforting Canadian dish with a tangy and crunchy texture, made with sour rye bread and a hint of yeast. Perfect for a quick snack or side dish.
instructions: Let the yeast dissolve in 1 cup of warm water for 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cubed bread, yeast mixture, caraway seeds, sea salt, and sugar. Let it sit for 30 minutes, allowing the bread to soak and the flavors to meld.
Add in the apple cider vinegar, black pepper, and grated ginger. Mix well to combine.
Pack the mixture into a clean 1-quart mason jar, pressing down on the bread to remove any air pockets.
Combine the fry ground rice, rice vinegar, and water in a small bowl. Pour the mixture over the bread in the mason jar.
Seal the jar and let it sit at room temperature for 24-48 hours, allowing the flavors to develop and the bread to become tangy and crunchy.
Store the pickles in the refrigerator to slow down the fermentation process.
Let the pickles sit for at least 4 hours before serving, allowing the flavors to meld and the bread to become even more tender.
To serve, scoop out a portion of the pickles and enjoy as a snack or side dish.
Note: The longer the pickles sit, the tangier and more sour they will become.
You can adjust the level of sourness to your liking by letting the pickles sit for a shorter or longer period of time.
You can also add other ingredients such as garlic, onions, or bell peppers to the pickles for added flavor.
Experiment with different seasonings and spices to create unique variations of the Sour Rye Pickle recipe.
